message:
fix: use default tmux socket and disarm respawn hooks before kill

Two issues caused unkillable zombie sessions:

1. gt derived a custom tmux socket name (e.g., gt-db5ab0) by default,
   creating sessions invisible to users on the default socket. When
   `gt deacon stop` killed them, the pane-died hook respawned them
   3 seconds later — infinite zombie loop.

2. KillSessionWithProcesses never disarmed the auto-respawn machinery
   (remain-on-exit + pane-died hook) before killing processes. This
   race condition affected ALL socket configurations.

Changes:
- Default to empty socket (default tmux server) instead of deriving
  per-town socket names. GT_TMUX_SOCKET override still works.
- Remove noTownSocket sentinel — empty socket correctly means "default"
- Disarm remain-on-exit and pane-died hook in KillSessionWithProcesses
  and KillSessionWithProcessesExcluding before touching processes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
